items.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
items.analysis: A study revealed that 282 iOS AI applications are insecurely handling API keys, leading to their leakage and open access to AI proxies in network traffic. This exposure creates risks for unauthorized AI service usage, data exfiltration, and potential account compromises.
items.tanium_hint: Look up the named iOS apps (or their bundle IDs) across managed mobile/endpoint inventory to see where they're installed; flag any on devices that touch corporate data.
items.rapid7_hint: Not a traditional network-scan target for mobile apps — instead research whether any exposed AI-proxy endpoints or leaked keys map to internet-facing services you scan.

items.title: Fake Perplexity extension on Chrome Web Store tracked searches
items.analysis: A malicious browser extension, disguised as Perplexity AI on the Chrome Web Store, was found to intercept and collect users' search queries and browsing data. This unauthorized data collection poses a significant privacy risk to affected individuals.
items.tanium_hint: Look up installed Chrome extensions across endpoints by extension ID; identify any machines carrying the impersonating extension.
items.rapid7_hint: Research whether the extension's callback domains appear in your scan/exposure data; browser extensions themselves aren't a scanner target, so focus on the exfil infrastructure.

items.title: Cyber insurance premiums climb as ransomware claims surge in Q2
items.analysis: Insurers reported a sharp rise in cyber insurance claims tied to ransomware in the second quarter, pushing premiums higher and tightening underwriting requirements. Brokers are advising clients to expect stricter security control attestations at renewal.
items.tanium_hint: Industry news — not a direct endpoint query. Useful for pulling endpoint coverage stats (EDR/patch state) that support control attestations underwriters ask for.
items.rapid7_hint: No direct scan action. Use scan/remediation SLA metrics as evidence for the control attestations increasingly required at policy renewal.

items.title: LockBit affiliate leaks stolen financial-sector data on new site
items.analysis: A LockBit affiliate published stolen data from several financial-sector victims on a newly stood-up leak site after ransom demands went unpaid. The dataset reportedly includes customer records and internal documents.
items.tanium_hint: Look up patch state and presence of the initial-access software LockBit affiliates are known to abuse (e.g. exposed remote-access and VPN products) across endpoints.
items.rapid7_hint: Confirm the CVEs tied to this actor's initial access are covered by your scan templates, and validate remediation on any matches.
